title: अय्यप्पा गायत्री
devanagariText:
॥ श्री अय्यप्पा गायत्री ॥

ॐ भूतनाथाय विद्महे भवपुत्राय धीमहि।
तन्नो अय्यप्पः प्रचोदयात्॥

ॐ शास्त्रे विद्महे महाशास्त्रे धीमहि।
तन्नो अय्यप्पः प्रचोदयात्॥

ॐ हरिहरपुत्राय विद्महे धर्मशास्त्राय धीमहि।
तन्नो शास्ता प्रचोदयात्॥

॥ इति श्री अय्यप्पा गायत्री सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Ayyappa Gayatri ||

Om Bhutanathaya Vidmahe Bhavaputraya Dhimahi.
Tanno Ayyappah Prachodayat.

Om Shastre Vidmahe Mahashastre Dhimahi.
Tanno Ayyappah Prachodayat.

Om Hariharaputraya Vidmahe Dharmashastraya Dhimahi.
Tanno Shasta Prachodayat.

|| Iti Shri Ayyappa Gayatri Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning: हम भूतनाथ (भूत-प्रेतों के स्वामी), भव (शिव) के पुत्र अय्यप्प का ध्यान करते हैं, अय्यप्प स्वामी हमारी बुद्धि को सन्मार्ग पर प्रेरित करें। हम शास्ता (नियामक) और महाशास्ता अय्यप्प का ध्यान करते हैं। हम हरि (विष्णु) और हर (शिव) के पुत्र, धर्मशास्ता अय्यप्प का ध्यान करते हैं, वे शास्ता हमें प्रेरणा प्रदान करें। अय्यप्पा गायत्री का पाठ ब्रह्मचर्य, धर्म और भक्ति को दृढ़ करता है।
benefits: Invokes the divine blessings of Lord Ayyappa (Dharma Shasta)
Invokes the blessing of discipline, celibacy, and righteous conduct
Traditionally recited for removal of fear of evil spirits and negative energies
Invokes courage, protection, and spiritual advancement
Especially beneficial during Mandala Kalam and Sabarimala pilgrimage
description: Dedicated to Lord Ayyappa, the Ayyappa Gayatri (अय्यप्पा गायत्री) is a powerful Vedic meditation mantra revered in Hindu tradition. Rooted in Kerala Tantra Tradition, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to bestow discipline, celibacy, and righteous conduct as well as remove fear of evil spirits and negative energies. Additionally, it is known to grant courage, protection, and spiritual advancement. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Saturday (Shanivar). The text contains 3 verses and takes around 2 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Ayyappa Gayatri in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Ayyappa Gayatri Mantra in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Invoke Lord Ayyappa's blessings for protection, discipline, and spiritual growth.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Ayyappa Gayatri Mantra?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ayyappa Gayatri (अय्यप्पा गायत्री) is a sacred Vedic-pattern 24-syllable meditation mantra invoking Lord Ayyappa — also known as Dharma Shasta, Hariharaputra (son of Hari/Vishnu and Hara/Shiva), Manikantha, Bhutanatha and Sabarinath — the presiding deity of Sabarimala temple in Kerala. The composition contains three Gayatri variants: (1) Om Bhutanathaya Vidmahe, Bhavaputraya Dhimahi, Tanno Ayyappah Prachodayat (invoking him as lord of all beings, son of Bhava/Shiva); (2) Om Shastre Vidmahe, Mahashastre Dhimahi, Tanno Ayyappah Prachodayat (invoking him as the Shasta, the disciplinarian/teacher); and (3) Om Hariharaputraya Vidmahe, Dharmashastraya Dhimahi, Tanno Shasta Prachodayat (invoking him as the Hari-Hara son and embodiment of dharma). (Source: Kerala Tantra tradition; Sabarimala paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Lord Ayyappa and what is the Hariharaputra story?
seoFaqs.answer: Lord Ayyappa is the divine son of Lord Vishnu (in his enchanting Mohini form) and Lord Shiva — hence the name Hariharaputra (son of Hari and Hara). The puranic narrative recounts that when Vishnu took the bewitching female form of Mohini to defeat Bhasmasura, Shiva became captivated by her beauty, and from their divine union was born Ayyappa to slay the demoness Mahishi (sister of Mahishasura). The infant was found by King Rajashekhara of Pandalam in Kerala on the banks of the Pampa river with a small bell around his neck — earning the name Manikantha (jeweled-neck). After fulfilling his mission, Ayyappa established his abode at Shabari Giri (Sabarimala) as the eternal naishtika brahmachari (strict celibate) and Dharma Shasta (lord of dharma). His Sabarimala temple draws over 50 million pilgrims annually. (Sources: Bhuta Natha Upakhyana; Brahmanda Purana; Pandalam royal tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the Ayyappa Gayatri most powerful to chant?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ayyappa Gayatri is most powerful on Saturday (Shanivar — Ayyappa's principal day, also the day to remove Shani-dosha through his grace), Monday (Somvar — sacred to Shiva his father), throughout the 41-day Mandala Kalam vrat (mid-November to late December — the principal Ayyappa vrat period), on Mandala Puja day (last day of the 41-day vrat), Makar Sankranti (January 14-15) coinciding with the world-famous Makaravilakku festival and Makara Jyoti darshan at Sabarimala, on Vishu (Kerala new year), and during the Pratistha Dinam of the Sabarimala temple. Devotees undertaking the Sabarimala pilgrimage (the over 50 million pilgrims annually) chant this Gayatri morning and evening throughout the 41-day vrat with the famous Saranam Ayyappa chant. (Sources: Sabarimala temple calendar; Drikpanchang Ayyappa festival dates.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the significance of the 41-day Mandala vrat for Ayyappa Gayatri recitation?
seoFaqs.answer: The Mandala Kalam is the 41-day vrat (vow) period observed by Ayyappa devotees, beginning from Vrishchika Sankramanam (mid-November when the sun enters Vrishchika/Scorpio) and culminating with Mandala Puja and Makara Jyoti darshan at Sabarimala in late December and on January 14 (Makar Sankranti). During these 41 days the devotee — who wears the irumudi (twin-sack) and the black or blue mundu, applies sandal and bhasma to forehead, wears tulsi or rudraksha mala — observes naishtika brahmacharya (strict celibacy), sleeps on the floor, eats one simple meal daily, abstains from meat-alcohol, performs morning and evening puja, and recites the Ayyappa Gayatri, Ashtottara, Bhujangam, Chalisa and the Saranam refrain (Swamiye Saranam Ayyappa). The discipline is the sole prerequisite for climbing the 18 sacred Pathinettam Padi steps to the sanctum. (Sources: Sabarimala Mandala vrat paddhati; Bhuta Natha Upakhyana.)

seoFaqs.question: Why are there three Gayatri variants in the Ayyappa Gayatri stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: The composition includes three Gayatri variants because Ayyappa is invoked under three of his most theologically loaded names — each Gayatri addressing a distinct aspect. The first Gayatri (Bhutanathaya Vidmahe, Bhavaputraya Dhimahi) invokes him as Bhutanatha (lord of all beings, including bhutas, pretas, pishachas — protective from supernatural beings) and Bhavaputra (son of Shiva). The second (Shastre Vidmahe, Mahashastre Dhimahi) invokes him as Shasta — the Lord-Disciplinarian who imposes dharma — and Mahashasta. The third (Hariharaputraya Vidmahe, Dharmashastraya Dhimahi) invokes him as the unique son of Hari (Vishnu) and Hara (Shiva) and the embodiment of Dharma-Shastra. Together the three Gayatris cover his protective role, his disciplining role and his unique parentage. (Source: Kerala Tantra tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Ayyappa Gayatri grant to devotees?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition attributes the following blessings to regular japa of the Ayyappa Gayatri: (1) divine protection from all forms of evil, especially bhutas, pretas, pishachas and tantric attacks — Ayyappa is supreme Bhutanatha; (2) discipline (vrata-niyama-shakti), naishtika brahmacharya and self-control — essential for the 41-day Mandala vrat; (3) safety during the difficult Sabarimala pilgrimage through forest paths; (4) removal of Shani-dosha and Saturday-related afflictions in the horoscope; (5) success in dharma-anushthana and righteous undertakings; (6) fulfilment of vows and prayers — Ayyappa is famously called Ishta-prada (giver of cherished desires) when approached with discipline; (7) eventual Shastuh-sayujya (eternal union with Lord Ayyappa) for the lifelong devotee; and (8) the supreme grace symbolised by the Makara Jyoti darshan. (Sources: Bhuta Natha Upakhyana phalashruti; Sabarimala Mandala paddhati.)
